rulings.Case Title: LARRY SUBLETT VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Intervenors
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 28, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Intervenors Maryland Casualty Co. and Fireman's Fund Insurance Co.'s, on behalf of defendant Marconi Plastering Company, Inc., motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY SUBLETT VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Devcon Construction Incorporated'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 28, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Devcon Construction Incorporated's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Defendant's evidence - Bret Sisney deposition testimony and declaration, defendant's Articles of Incorporation, Dana Bennett's declaration and plaintiff's responses to other defendants' discovery - does not negate the testimony of Robert Cantley. Specifically, it does not demonstrate that defendant did not perform work at the Lockheed Sunnyvale campus in the mid-1970s nor does it demonstrate that the products/materials to which defendant's employees exposed asbestos did not contain asbestos. At most, when read together, this evidence merely creates a triable issue for the jury. Additionally, although it does not appear that defendant attempted to sustain its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar, if it had it would be precluded from doing so because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Robert Cantley's deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
